title: Refactor OCP-069 (OCP): Large if-else if chain on stack object types, requiring modification for new...

detected_issue.description: Large if-else if chain on stack object types, requiring modification for new internal types or JsonElement types.
detected_issue.reasoning: The `peek` method contains a long `if/else if` chain that inspects the type of the object on top of the internal stack (`o instanceof Iterator`, `o instanceof JsonObject`, `o instanceof JsonArray`, `o instanceof JsonPrimitive`, `o instanceof JsonNull`). Furthermore, the `JsonPrimitive` branch has nested `if/else if` checks. If a new internal stack object type or a new `JsonElement` subclass were introduced, this method would need to be modified to include new `else if` branches. This is a clear OCP violation, as extending the internal representation or JSON element types requires altering this core method.

fix_logic.summary: Refactor JsonTreeReader.peek() to use a visitor-like pattern for JsonElement types
fix_logic.rationale: The long if-else chain in peek() violates OCP by requiring modifications for every new JsonElement type. By delegating the token determination to the JsonElement itself (or a helper), we decouple the reader from the specific internal structure of the elements.
